feat(frontend): install and configure shadcn ui with Tailwind v4, add…
add the react forntend project setup
Update title in index.html to 'HR Tracker Management System'
fix: improve error message for invalid commands (issue #2611)
prs.body:
## Pull Request Template

**Description:**

- Improved the error message displayed when running an invalid command in the GoFr CLI.
- Addresses Issue #2611.
- Motivation: The previous error message "No Command Found!" was misleading as it implied the application had no commands at all. The new message `'<entered-command>' is not a valid command.` provides clear feedback to the user about which specific command was not recognized, followed by the list of available commands. This improves the CLI user experience.

**Breaking Changes (if applicable):**

- None. This change only updates the error message string for invalid commands.

**Additional Information:**

- Modified [pkg/gofr/cmd.go](cci:7://file:///Users/bhavishyasharma/Desktop/gofr/pkg/gofr/cmd.go:0:0-0:0) to include the invalid command name in the [ErrCommandNotFound](cci:2://file:///Users/bhavishyasharma/Desktop/gofr/pkg/gofr/cmd.go:28:0-30:1) struct and formatted the error string.
- Updated [pkg/gofr/cmd_test.go](cci:7://file:///Users/bhavishyasharma/Desktop/gofr/pkg/gofr/cmd_test.go:0:0-0:0) to assert the new error message format in unit tests.

**Checklist:**

- [x] I have formatted my code using `goimport` and `golangci-lint`.
- [x] All new code is covered by unit tests.
- [x] This PR does not decrease the overall code coverage.
- [x] I have reviewed the code comments and documentation for clarity.

**Thank you for your contribution!**

prs.title: feat: add gRPC auth middleware and enable standard OTEL tracing
prs.body:
## Pull Request Template

**Description:**

- I've been working on closing the gap between our gRPC and HTTP services (Issue #2576).
- This PR brings **Authentication** to gRPC. I added interceptors for Basic Auth, API Keys, and OAuth so they match what we already have for HTTP.
- I also tweaked the gRPC logger to handle standard OpenTelemetry tracing headers. It tries to use the standard OTEL way first, which makes it much easier to integrate with other tools.

**Breaking Changes (if applicable):**

- Nope, no breaking changes.
- The tracing update is backward compatible—if it doesn't find the standard headers, it falls back to our custom ones.

**Additional Information:**

- I put all the new auth logic in `pkg/gofr/grpc/middleware`.
- Added a bunch of tests in `auth_test.go` to cover the new interceptors.

**Checklist:**

- [x] I have formatted my code using `goimport` and `golangci-lint`.
- [x] All new code is covered by unit tests.
- [x] This PR does not decrease the overall code coverage.
- [x] I have reviewed the code comments and documentation for clarity.

**Thank you for your contribution!**

The chnages that I made are listed below:

1. Title & Icons: Added an engaging title with an emoji and icons for each section header to enhance readability.

2. Introduction Paragraph: Added a brief welcoming description to explain the repository's purpose.

3. Section Formatting: Used quotes and highlights for key information and added sub-sections under Requirements for clarity.

4. Improved Links: Made URLs clickable and clarified certain requirements (like needing an account on koji.katello.org).

5. Visual Enhancements in License Section: Used an icon and emphasized the MIT License for quick recognition.

prs.title: Update README.md
prs.body:
## Updates Made to the README

1. **Project Title and Logo**: Added a title and logo for visual identity.
2. **Welcome Message**: Introduced a friendly welcome highlighting the project's purpose.
3. **Table of Contents**: Included for easy navigation to relevant sections.
4. **Detailed Project Benefits**: Expanded the "Why Choose This Project?" section to emphasize learning and community support.
5. **Features Section**: Enhanced with bullet points and emojis, added stickers for visual appeal.
6. **Technologies Used**: Listed technologies to clarify the project's technical stack.
7. **Getting Started Instructions**: Streamlined installation process with clear steps and emojis.
8. **Contribution Guidelines**: Added step-by-step instructions for contributing.
9. **Suggestions for Enhancements**: Included ideas for potential project improvements.
10. **License Information**: Clearly stated the MIT License for user rights.
11. **Support Section**: Encouraged users to star the repository for community engagement.
12. **Connection Invitation**: Invited users to connect and provide feedback.
